title: orthiopteris caudata
intro: Orthiopteris caudata, also known as the Australian maidenhair fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Orthiopteris caudata include the potential treatment of various conditions, such as fever, cough, and digestive issues, as well as its use as an anti-inflammatory agent to reduce pain and swelling. The plant is typically prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or as a poultice, making it a valuable addition to traditional remedies in some parts of the world.
